title: Git pre-commit hook for structural indexing of staged source files
purpose: Processes staged source files by scanning them for structural information, updating a pseudo-database, and ensuring the database changes are committed with the current changes.
module_context: This module is a command-line script executed by a git pre-commit hook. It uses child process execution to interact with git, manages file system operations for logging and database updates, and integrates with source scanning and pseudo-database services.

steps.content: Ensure the log directory exists for the project.
Construct the path to the structural index log file.
Append the error message with timestamp to the log file.
Silently ignore any failures during logging.
Execute git command to list modified and added files in the staged changes.
Parse the output of the git command into an array of modified file paths.
Execute git command to list deleted files in the staged changes.
Parse the output of the git command into an array of deleted file paths.
IF git commands fail, log the error and return empty arrays.
Retrieve the list of modified and deleted staged files.
IF there are no modified or deleted files, exit the process.
Initialize the pseudo-database instance for the project.
Process each modified file by checking its extension and scanning its content.
IF scanning succeeds, upsert the structural data into the pseudo-database.
IF scanning fails, log the error and continue with the next file.
Process each deleted file by checking its extension and removing its data from the pseudo-database.
IF deletion fails, log the error and continue with the next file.
Checkpoint the write-ahead log of the pseudo-database.
Stage the updated database file in git for inclusion in the commit.
Log the number of files updated and removed from the index.
Exit the process with success code.
